Coinbase offers a Coinbase Business account that is designed specifically for businesses. With a Coinbase Business account, you can securely buy and sell cryptocurrencies, manage multiple users and wallets, and view detailed reports on your activity.   1. Go to the Coinbase website and click on the "Sign Up" button.

Once you have completed the registration process, you can use your Coinbase Business account to buy and sell cryptocurrencies, manage multiple users and wallets, and view detailed reports on your activity.

Items.Title: 内部部署还是云存储？ 教育机构的前进方向 | On-Premise or Cloud Storage? The way forward for educational institutions
Items.Description:
世界上绝大多数教育机构已经开始在某种程度上以电子方式存储有关学生人口统计、出勤率、评分、考试分数和其他管理信息的数据。 这些机构通常将数据存储在本地或云端。 但是哪个更好呢？ 您如何保证这些数据的安全？ 选择供应商时应遵循哪些准则？ 我们将回答所有这些问题，以帮助您为您的机构做出最佳决定。

本地存储是指在机构校园内安装和运行计算机系统，而不是在远程位置。 本地存储的一些优势包括加强对所有系统和数据的控制以及对学生信息的内部处理。 但是，本地存储需要专职人员进行维护和支持。 不久之前，本地存储是最常见的存储类型。 然而，在过去十年中，随着基于云的解决方案的兴起，这种情况发生了变化。 对于选择内部部署方法的机构，我们建议在云环境中包括备份存储。 云计算是一种数据存储模型，其中存储的信息跨越多个服务器（通常是位置），物理环境通常由第三方公司拥有和管理。 与本地存储不同，云计算有许多优势，包括成本优化的服务、有限的软件许可成本、没有新的基础设施要求、存储公司承担大部分工作并无需额外的 IT 人员等。

近来，教育数据隐私和学生隐私保护受到越来越多的关注。 虽然这是云计算精神中的一个热门话题，但许多教育工作者并不熟悉保护学生数据的复杂性。 由于组织决策者通常不知道与云计算中的数据保护相关的程序，因此机构应执行以下步骤以确保其学生数据安全：

维护物理措施以保护网络设备的物理位置，包括访问服务器机房和存储区域网络等网络中心的多步骤过程。

  • 在校园内建立单独的网络接入点，将行政和教学人员的网络接入与学生或访客隔离开来。
  • 我们将数据库管理员权限限制在必要的最少人数——通常是主要和次要数据库员工，如管理员或经理。
  • 进行例行的安全审计和网络渗透测试，以确保既定的安全措施符合当前的合规性和网络安全标准。
  • 建立灾难恢复策略，以与存储在内部部署的数据相同的保护级别保护外部安全的数据。
  • 密切监控网络流量以检测并阻止任何异常或剥削活动。

随着教育机构迅速转向基于云的存储，许多重要流程很容易被遗忘，因为云服务可能知之甚少、不透明且管理不力。 据报道，相当多的机构甚至在其云合同文档中存在很大差距，包括缺少隐私或数据共享政策。

此外，机构在选择云服务时经常放弃对学生信息的控制。 例如，很少有第三方协议规定披露学生信息的目的和范围，限制供应商销售或营销此类共享信息的协议就更少了。 许多协议还允许供应商更改服务和隐私条款，恕不另行通知。

鉴于某些第三方协议中提供的控制有限，正在考虑采用基于云的数据存储方法的机构在选择云计算提供商时应遵循以下准则。

  • 需要一份包括所有各方并定义安全要求的单一合同，以确保管理和学生数据的保护。
  • 确保云提供商遵循推荐的合规性标准。
  • 确保适当的云数据库管理和监控。
  • 确保定期进行数据库更新和应用程序更新活动。
  • 通过冗余故障转移的规定维护多个数据中心。
  • 认真监控行业标准，以确保遵循最新的保护和安全建议。

总之，虽然云或混合模式是前进的方向，但提高机构工作人员和政策制定者对数据存储的认识对于确保数据安全和隐私至关重要。 这些关于保护教育数据的适当隐私措施的讨论的出现将有助于大学和学院加强其内部管理程序、数据管理程序和存储方法。
所有这些措施似乎难以实施，但值得庆幸的是，Taashee Linux Services（CMMI 成熟度 3 级评估和 ISO 认证）是 AWS 咨询和技术合作伙伴，我们随时为您提供帮助。 凭借在 AWS 和 MS Azure 基于云的解决方案方面十多年的集体经验，Taashee 是许多全球企业在实施具有成本效益和高效的云解决方案方面的首选合作伙伴。

原文:

A vast majority of educational institutions across the world have started, on some level or the other, to electronically store data regarding student demographics, attendance, grading, examination scores, and additional administrative information. These institutions generally store data either on-premise or in the cloud. But which one is better? How can you keep this data safe? And what are the guidelines to follow when choosing a vendor? We will answer all these questions to help you make the best decision for your institution.

On-premise storage refers to the installation and operation of computer systems on an institution’s campus, as opposed to a remote location. Some advantages of on-premise storage include increased control over all systems and data as well as internal handling of student information. However, on-premise storage requires dedicated staff for maintenance and support. Not too long ago, on-premise storage was the most common type of storage; however, that has changed in the past decade with the rise of cloud-based solutions. For institutions that opt for an on-premise approach, we recommend including backup storage in a cloud environment. Taashee’s disaster recovery service for on-premise storage includes a rapid start-up service that is capable of restoring an institution’s information within 24 hours if the on-premise solution is compromised.

Cloud computing is a model of data storage where the stored information spans multiple servers (and often locations) and the physical environment is typically owned and managed by a third-party company. Unlike on-premise storage, cloud computing has many advantages, including cost-optimized services, limited software licensing costs, no new infrastructure requirements, the storage company doing most of the work and eliminating the need for extra IT staff, etc.

Recently, there has been increased attention on education data privacy and the protection of student privacy. While this is a popular topic within the cloud computing ethos, many educators are unfamiliar with the complexities of protecting student data. Because the procedures associated with the protection of data within cloud computing are generally unknown to organizational policymakers, institutions should go through the following steps to ensure their student data is safe:

Maintaining physical measures to secure physical locations of network equipment, including multistep processes to access network centers such as server rooms and storage area networks.

  • Establishing separate network access points within campuses that segregate network access of administrative and teaching staff from students or guests.
  • We are limiting database administrator rights to an essential minimum number of people—typically primary and secondary database staff such as administrators or managers.
  • Conduct routine security audits and network penetration testing to ensure that the established security measures meet current compliance and cybersecurity standards.
  • Establishing a disaster recovery policy that can protect data secured off-premise at the same level of protection as data stored on-premise.
  • Monitoring network traffic closely to detect and block any unusual or exploitative activity.

As educational institutions rapidly shift to cloud-based storage, many vital processes are easily forgotten, since cloud services can be poorly understood, non-transparent, and weakly governed. A sizable number of institutions reportedly even have wide gaps in their cloud contract documentation, including missing privacy or data-sharing policies.

Moreover, institutions frequently surrender control of student information when opting for cloud services. For example, very few third-party agreements specify the purpose and extent for disclosure of student information and even fewer such agreements restrict the sale or marketing of this shared information by vendors. Many agreements also allow vendors to change the terms of service and privacy without notice.

Given the limited control given in some third-party agreements, institutions that are considering a cloud-based data storage approach should go through the following guidelines when selecting a cloud-computing provider.

  • Requiring a single contract that includes all parties and defines security requirements ensuring the protection of administrative and student data.
  • Ensuring that the cloud provider follows recommended compliance standards.
  • Ensuring proper cloud database management and monitoring.
  • Ensuring database updating and application updating activities are undertaken regularly.
  • Maintaining multiple data centers with provisions for redundant failover.
  • Diligently monitoring industry standards to ensure the latest protection and security recommendations are being followed.

In conclusion, although the cloud or a hybrid model is the way forward, increased awareness of institution staff and policymakers about data storage is paramount for ensuring data security and privacy. The emergence of these discussions regarding proper privacy measures to protect educational data will help universities and colleges strengthen their internal administrative procedures, data management procedures, and storage methodologies.
All these measures may seem overwhelming to implement, but thankfully Taashee Linux Services (CMMI Maturity Level 3 Appraised and ISO Certified), is an AWS Consulting and Technology Partner and we are here to help you out. With more than a decade of collective experience in AWS and MS Azure cloud-based solutions, Taashee is a partner of choice for many global businesses in implementing cost-effective and highly efficient cloud solutions.

If you're a developer who writes articles on daily.dev, you may want to display these articles on your personal blog or portfolio website. One way to do this is by using the dev.to API, which allows you to access articles from daily.dev and other websites.

Step 1: Get Your daily.dev Username

First, you'll need to find your daily.dev username. This is the handle that appears in the URL of your articles, such as "https://dev.to/{username}/your-article-slug".

Step 2: Fetch Your Articles

To fetch your articles from daily.dev, make a GET request to the following endpoint:

https://dev.to/api/articles?username={USERNAME}

Replace {USERNAME} with your actual daily.dev username. This endpoint will return a JSON array of your articles, along with their titles, URLs, and other metadata.

Step 3: Display Your Articles in Your Blog App

Once you have your articles, you can display them in your blog app however you like. For example, you could loop through the array of articles and render a list of links to each article. Or, you could display the full text of each article on a separate page.

To display the full text of an article, you can make a GET request to the URL of the article, which is included in the JSON object returned by the API. This will return the HTML content of the article, which you can then parse and display in your app.

Conclusion

Using the dev.to API, it's easy to fetch and display your articles from daily.dev in your personal blog or portfolio website. With just a few lines of code, you can keep your app up to date with your latest content from daily.dev.

Items.Title: 人工智能在检测网络钓鱼攻击中的作用 | The Role of Artificial Intelligence in Detecting Phishing Attacks
Items.Description:
网络钓鱼是一种复杂的社会工程攻击，旨在窃取受害者的敏感信息，例如信用卡或社会安全号码以及其他敏感数据。 为了使电子邮件看起来像是来自可信来源，大多数网络钓鱼攻击都采用伪装电子邮件的形式。 该邮件的主题行通常带有紧急消息，要求立即采取行动，例如，通知帐户已被盗用或服务即将暂停。

攻击者的主要目的是警告收件人并提示立即采取行动，主要是下载附件或点击链接而不考虑他们在做什么。 一次点击可能会导致恶意软件感染，从而导致您的私人信息被盗。

这种骗局对组织来说确实令人担忧，也是网络安全领域的主要威胁。 但是，要检测和防止网络钓鱼，有多种工具和技术。 检测和防止网络钓鱼的最佳工具之一是人工智能 (AI)。 它以算法为基础，侧重于从大量数据中理解和识别模式，以创建一个可以预测异常行为和异常的系统。 它在学习正常行为模式的同时随着时间的推移而发展。 您一定在想人工智能 (AI) 将如何检测和防止网络钓鱼攻击？ 人工智能可以采取哪些方式来应对网络钓鱼攻击？ 这个博客有你所有问题的答案。

让我们继续吧！

人工智能 (AI) 如何检测网络钓鱼攻击？

众所周知，人工智能 (AI) 可以使电子邮件分析更快、更准确，但这里出现的主要问题是，AI 究竟如何检测和预防网络钓鱼？

一个基于人工智能和机器学习的成功系统是在整个电子邮件（从元数据到消息内容）中寻找异常情况和网络钓鱼警告信号的系统。 例如，这包括基于电子邮件行为（例如伪造发件人）和消息意图（例如紧急主题）的警报。

网络钓鱼攻击的主要迹象之一是邮件中的紧迫感。 如果您收到一封需要快速操作的电子邮件，并且其中的文字显示出紧急性，警告信号就会亮起。 然后，人工智能 (AI) 会检测并理解邮件的上下文，检查它是普通垃圾邮件、网络钓鱼攻击还是合法邮件。

我们以“宣传”一词为例。 就其本身而言，这个词可能已经被认为是可疑的。 然而，人工智能系统试图更准确地了解电子邮件是否真的是一种威胁，以及这种威胁的严重程度。 这样可以更好地区分诸如“紧急：30% OFF 宣传”（在本例中表示简单的垃圾邮件）和“立即填写您的卡号以参与宣传”（在本例中表示网络钓鱼骗局）。

确切的逻辑适用于电子邮件标题中的警告标志。 例如，AI 可以识别电子邮件欺骗（伪造的发件人）、拼写错误的域以及其他类型的欺骗。 该系统与 SPF、DKIM 和 DMARC 等传统引擎相结合，显着扩展了威胁检测功能。

通过这种方式，将电子邮件作为一个整体进行分析，而不是仅仅与其他现有的网络钓鱼诈骗进行比较。

例如，系统将考虑是否存在先前的对话、发件人可能在电子邮件中请求的任何数据、主题中所写的主题与消息本身之间的联系等等。

除此之外，为了使分析高度准确，基于人工智能的电子邮件保护不断从数据以及用户和管理员的反馈中学习。

如今，使用传统的电子邮件检测工具和安全解决方案检测欺诈并不容易。 特别是，当今对企业伤害最大的欺诈类型很难被发现。 这样做的原因是它不涉及恶意电子邮件的典型元素。

通过一个一个地打开一个对话通道，黑客可以发送可信的电子邮件，直到他或她让这个人或目标执行所需的操作。 例如，提供机密性、sata 或敏感数据、支付账单、进行银行转账、下载恶意文件或恶意软件。

有必要使用人工智能 (AI) 算法来预防和检测这些可以理解用户交流方式的专门骗局。 例如，了解他们的典型行为、文本模式以及消息的上下文是否有意义。

例如，如果有人想模仿公司的首席执行官，系统必须能够识别高管沟通模式的变化，并在攻击造成任何损害之前阻止攻击。

人工智能 (AI) 应对网络钓鱼攻击的 4 种方式
当机器人工智能停止适应时，它是唯一失败的例子。 由于技术发展每天都在更新，因此请注意以下信息以缩小知识差距并应对攻击。

  • 有必要研究来自端点设备的数据并预测任何可能性，而不是在执行后分析事情。 这将有助于在正确的时间防止任何复杂的破坏尝试。

包起来
如上所述，人工智能 (AI) 是检测和预防网络钓鱼的重要工具。 通常，它使用数据分析和机器学习来检查元数据、内容、上下文和典型的用户行为。 通过这种方式，它可以快速准确地识别潜在威胁并防止它们窃取您的敏感数据。

原文:

Phishing is a sophisticated social engineering attack that is created to steal the sensitive information of the victim such as credit cards or social security numbers and other sensitive data. To make the email look like it comes from trusted sources, most phishing attacks take the form of emails disguised. The subject line of that mail generally carries an urgent message in which it is required to drive immediate action, for instance, notice that an account has been compromised or that a service is about to be suspended.

The main aim of the attacker is to alarm the recipient and prompt immediate action, mainly downloading an attachment or clicking on a link without thinking about what they’re doing. That one click can result in a malware infection, which can lead to your private information getting stolen.

This scam is really worrisome for organizations as well as a major threat in the cybersecurity world. However, to detect and prevent phishing, there are several tools and techniques. One of the best tools for detecting and preventing phishing is artificial intelligence (AI). It works on algorithm basis and focuses on understanding as well as recognizing patterns from vast piles of data to create a system that can predict unusual behavior and anomalies. It evolves with time while learning patterns of normal behavior. These features of AI make it helpful in identifying phishing emails, spam, and malware.

You must be thinking How Artificial Intelligence (AI) will detect and prevent phishing attacks? What are the several ways AI can take on phishing attacks? This blog has answers to all your questions.

Let’s proceed!

How Does Artificial Intelligence (AI) Detects Phishing Attacks?

As we all know that artificial intelligence (AI) can make email analysis faster as well as more accurate, but the main question arises here is that How exactly does AI work to detect and prevent phishing?

1. Looking for Anomalies and Warning Signals
A successful system based on artificial intelligence and machine learning is one that looks for anomalies as well as warning signals for phishing throughout the email, from the metadata to the message content. This includes, for instance, alerts based both on email behavior (e.g. forged senders) and message intent (such as urgent topics).

One of the main signs of a phishing attack is the sense of urgency in the message. If you got an email that requires quick action as well as have words that show urgency, the warning signal is lit. Artificial Intelligence (AI) then works to detect as well as understand the context of the message, checking if it is a common spam, a phishing attack or a legitimate message.

Let’s take the word “publicity” as an instance. In itself, the word may already be considered suspicious. However, an AI system seeks to understand more precisely whether or not the email is really a threat and how serious that threat is. This allows for a better distinction between phrases such as “Urgent: 30% OFF publicity” (in this case, indicating a simple spam) and “Fill in your card number right now to participate in the publicity” (in this case, indicating a phishing scam).

The exact logic applies to warning signs in the email header. For instance, AI identifies cases of email spoofing (forged senders), misspelled domains as well as other types of spoofing. The system significantly extends threat detection capabilities coupled with traditional engines such as SPF, DKIM and DMARC.

2. Analyzing the Message Context
AI has an ability to examine a message based on context which is another important point that makes AI one of the biggest defenses against phishing. In this way, an email is analyzed as a whole instead of just compared to other existing phishing scams.

The system will take into consideration, for instance, the presence or absence of a previous conversation, any data that the sender may be requesting in the email, the connection between the topic written in the subject and the message itself, among others.

Apart from this, to make the analysis highly accurate, AI-based email protection constantly learns from the data and the users and administrators feedback.

3. Understanding How Users Communicate
Nowadays, it is not easy to detect the frauds with the use of traditional email detection tools and security solutions. Specially, the type of fraud that hurts businesses the most today is very hard to detect. The reason for this is that it doesn’t involve the typical elements of a malicious email.

By opening a dialogue channel one by one, the hacker can send credible emails until he or she gets the person or target to perform a desired action. For instance, providing confidentiality, sata or sensitive data, making a bill payment, making a bank transfer, downloading a malicious file or malware.

It is necessary to use Artificial intelligence (AI) algorithms in preventing and detecting these specialized scams that can understand how users communicate. For instance, knowing their typical behavior, their textual patterns, as well as whether the context of the messages makes sense.

If there is anyone who wants to imitate the CEO of the company, for instance, the system must be able to identify changes in the executive’s communication pattern and block the attack before it causes any damage.

4 Ways Artificial Intelligence (AI) Can Take on Phishing Attacks
When Machine Artificial Intelligence stops adapting is the only instance when it fails. As technical development is updated daily, it is useful to note the below information to narrow down the knowledge gap and counter the attacks.

  • 1. To identify real-time threats, one should introduce Artificial Intelligence (AI) algorithms on every mobile phone, even on offline devices.
  • 2. To deduct the unnecessary hassle of unsecured passwords, these algorithms can help strengthen the security of mobile phones and double as an employee identification card.
  • 3. The evaluation of the efficacy of a cyber security strategy will be based on an enterprise’s ability to predict, comply, and deter threats.
  • 4. One should increase cyber security protection in a predictive manner. It is necessary to study the data from endpoint devices and predict any possibility instead of analyzing things after they have been executed. This will help to prevent any sophisticated breaching attempts at the right time.

Wrapping Up
As mentioned above, Artificial Intelligence (AI) is an important tool in detecting and preventing phishing. Usually, it uses data analysis and machine learning to examine metadata, content, context, and typical user behavior. In this way, it quickly and accurately identifies potential threats and prevents them from stealing your sensitive data.
